post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 246 (Newbie Game 97)

Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Read all the rules!


Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by naming one of Santa's reindeer. Do not repeat one that was used for someone's previous confirmation.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: fdas
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (fdas) am the moderator of Mafia Game 246
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons


Role PMs
Quote:
You are an Ordinary Elf (Vanilla Townie) that works in Santa's factory, diligently making toys. You have no special powers but each day you can help discuss with the others to try to identify the grinches.

You win when all of the Grinches (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Eustace Yulelog (Cop), the record manager at Santa's factory. During the night you may examine the background of one of the individuals working in the factory and determine whether or not they are secretly a grinch.

You win when all of the Grinches (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Manius Tannenbaum (Doctor), the elf that Santa placed in charge of taking care of needs of the elves. During the night you may check up on someone and save them if they are attacked by the grinches. You may not target yourself two nights in a row.

You win when all of the Grinches (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Cornelius Candycane (Tracker), Santa's factory manager. Santa has entrusted you to help keep everything smoothly running. Every night you may investigate someone's activities to determine who they visited during the night.

You win when all of the Grinches (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Claudius Bellringer (Jailkeeper), the security officer in charge of the factory. Each night you may choose someone to lock up in a cell, preventing them from performing any actions that night while also preventing them from being killed.

You win when all of the Grinches (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are Moultrie Mistletoe (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie), an elf working in Santa's factory. You are smart and able to wiggle out of dangerous situations. On the first time that you are attacked by the Grinches, that attack will be negated.

You win when all of the Grinches (Mafia) have been eliminated.
Quote:
You are a Grinch (Mafia Goon) who wants to ruin Christmas. You have infiltrated the North Pole and have disguised yourself among the elves. Now, your task is to kill off all of the elves to stop toys from being made. You may talk with your partner () here to discuss who you want to kill.

You win when all of the Elves (Town) are dead.
Quote:
You are an Elder Grinch (Mafia Roleblocker) who wants to ruin Christmas. You have infiltrated the North Pole and have disguised yourself among the elves. Now, your task is to kill off all of the elves to stop toys from being made. You may talk with your partner () here to discuss who you want to kill. You may also target someone and place traps next to them and that night, the target will be unable to perform any actions. Your ability takes precedence over the jailkeeper.

You win when all of the Elves (Town) are dead.



Alive (4)
mathjay+ midwaybear+
Scipio1+
TheFlamingoHacker iamc8power ad333
nemoatl walrus_fangs MaverickMan


Dead(5)
SeverusSlouch (Vanilla Townie)
EulerNTM (Mafia Goon)
ssbgm9002 (Vanilla Townie)
galaxy1220 (Vanilla Townie)
NDMath++ (Jailkeeper)


Events
Day 1: SeverusSlouch modkilled
Night 1: No one died
Day 2: EulerNTM hanged
Night 2: ssbgm died
Day 3: galaxy1220 hanged
Night 3: NDMath died


PM Prods
 


Warnings
SeverusSlouch: editing
walrus_fangs: editing


Do not post until this line is crossed out

While the original grinch turned from evil and came to love Christmas, the legacy he left in the public consciousness continued to persist. Those people who despise Christmas turn into evil spirits known as grinches after death, tirelessly working to undermine the Christmas holiday. They are a threat that Santa and his elves must remain ever vigilante to make sure the evildoers do not succeed in ruining the happiness of children around the world.

As Christmas began to approach this year, Santa boarded his sleigh and began to make his rounds to deliver presents. His sleigh crashed not long after leaving the north pole, with the cause quickly determined to be sabotage. It was clear that the grinches had infiltrated the north pole and would stop at nothing to ruin Christmas. Worse yet, Santa had crashed in territory controlled by the Soviet Union, which had outlawed Christmas and considered Santa to be the enemy of the proletariat. The situation at the north pole had to be dealt with quickly so that a rescue party could be sent to help Santa Claus before the Soviets discover him.

Faced with these dire circumstances, the elves working in Santa's factory at the north pole knew they had to do something quickly to protect Christmas. Meanwhile, the grinches began to make their move, killing off elves one by one while they slept.


disclaimer
I do not claim credit for inventing these character.


Day 1 begins. Current time: 20:00:00 EST 25 January 2021.

post_rendered:
obligatory newbie copypasta

Hello newbies! I'm here to help you guys out with this game.
First of all, READ THE IMPORTANT RULES TAB. As long as you can follow these rules everything should be fine. If you have any trouble determining what a rule means (e.g. OOT can be hard to understand as a newbie) then ask fdas, me, or awesomeming.

Also, make sure to confirm. If you thoroughly read all of the rules, then you should have seen a message that tells you how to confirm. Although it may not exactly say "do <something> to confirm", if you keep a sharp eye out you should see something out of place. That will be the confirm rule's instructions!

MOST IMPORTANT RULES TO REMEMBER

These rules are usually the ones that newbies mess up the most, so make sure to try and follow these rules above all else.
  • DO NOT SPOIL THE CONFIRM RULE.
  • DO NOT SPEAK OF THIS GAME OUTSIDE OF THE THREAD. (Tip: Pretend this game doesn't exist when posting anywhere else in AoPS)
  • DO NOT EDIT OR DELETE YOUR POSTS. (Due to game integrity purposes, editing and deleting posts are not allowed)
  • DO NOT MAKE PERSONAL ATTACKS. (This is saying stuff like "how did you make it pass grade school with these social skills" and the like)
  • DO NOT POST IF YOU ARE DEAD OR POST DURING THE NIGHT.
  • DON'T TAKE WORDS TO HEART. (Sometimes people may say slightly rude things, but in the end they don't really mean it. Just remind yourself that this is a game and if you get heated take a break)
  • MAKE SURE TO FORMAT YOUR VOTES CORRECTLY. (Read the rules for voting if you are unsure how to vote)

And that's it! Let the games begin.

Guides:

Voting/Hanging FAQ - PLEASE READ
Why do we not No Hang on Day 1?

Because we lose the ability to eliminate an extra person.
Here's the math:
(I'm going under the assumption that we mishang, or wrongly hang, a townie every time we hang per day.)

7-2 (7 townspeople, 2 mafia)
We no hang D1.
Scum (other term for mafia) kills a townsperson.
6-2
We hang a townsperson.
Scum kills a townie (other term for townsperson/people).
4-2
We hang another townie.
Scum kills a townie.
2-2 (Town loses! Reminder that if the number of scum equals or is greater than the number of townies left, scum wins)
In this theoretical scenario, we only use 2 hangs before we lose.
However, look at when we don't no hang D1.

7-2 (7 townspeople, 2 mafia)
We hang D1.
Scum kills N1.
5-2
We hang D2.
Scum kills N2.
3-2
We hang a townie D3.
2-2
As you can see, we use up 3 hangs this time instead of two, so it is just mechanically better to hang D1.

What is LyLo and MyLo?

LyLo is hang or lose and MyLo is mishang or lose. Simply put, LyLo is when town must hang scum in order to survive, and MyLo is when it is mechanically better (although sometimes not the best choice) for town to no hang to bring town to LyLo. For example, 3-2 is LyLo and 4-2 is MyLo. In MyLo, when town no hangs scum is forced to kill a person which can narrow down the options for who is scum.

What are locked votes and why do they matter?

Locked votes mean that once you cast a vote, you cannot change it. This will be applied in LyLo of this game, so make sure to be cautious once you enter a LyLo situation, because once you make your vote it is permanent.

What's the difference between majority and deadline hang?

Majority hang is when if town reaches a majority vote on one player, that player will be hanged before deadline ends.
Deadline hang is when the leading wagon at the end of the day is hanged even if the wagon reached majority before deadline.

So, we cannot quickly hang someone on D1 and LyLo but we can on D2 and other days (that are not LyLo).


aroodman's op guide - please read

How to Hunt scum - Do not need to read but it will help even if you just skim it
No this is not copypasta'ed from MU, I made it myself.

Scumhunting is usually portrayed as complicated and rigorous, especially to new newbies of the forum. However, it actually relies mostly on intuition and processing actions people make in the game. There are a few basic fundamentals that anyone who plays mafia should learn and more importantly, understand. Understanding why these tells work and how to put them to use allows you to combine tells or slightly alter them depending on the context of which you are applying these scum tells in. With this basic toolkit, you can master Mafia by applying them in games and practicing them frequently.

Please read the hide tags which explain these scumtells, as you may misuse these tells if you take them for granted.

I. Experience
The most powerful tool of mafia is experience, and applying patterns you see in different games allows you to easily recognize the difference between scum and town.


II. Motivation tells
Scum want to kill townspeople and find ways to hang town instead of scum, whether as townies want to kill scum. If you put yourself in another person's shoes and consider why they made a specific post, you may be able to gain insight on if this person is town or scum (This tell is referenced as a Motivation tell).


III. Night Kill Analysis
Tread carefully with NKA! There are times when NKA is a dangerous ground to study, and there can be lots of WIFOM involved.

Night kills are a way for scum to quickly eliminate town, but townies (townspeople, vanilla townies, etc.) can also glean information from NK's (night kills). There are infinitely many reasons for why scum could NK a specific person, however consider probability over possibility, as scum are far less likely to actually kill a person for a random reason like "he/she thought that I was mafia from this one post at the very beginning of the game". Consider each of your suspects making the kill, and see if it makes some sense for them to kill this person. Killing that person for the sake of killing them is not good logic and you must think probability over possibility. (This tell is referenced as Night Kill Analysis, or NKA)


IV. PoE
Sometimes finding town is more effective then finding scum. If you successfully identify every townie, then the remaining people who you don't think are a townie must be scum. "How do you find townies?" is a common question, and the straightforward answer is that you must apply all of the other scumhunting tells but to try and identify if a person is town instead of trying to identify if a person is scum. (This process is called PoE)


V. Meta
Meta is not as important or as powerful as any of the other tells in this list. Some people have used it as a crutch to make their reads, but do not follow their example. Meta is only used to support a read lightly with previous games, as patterns can change and habit can disappear if the player you are analyzing makes these changes in the ongoing game.

Make sure that you do not OOT with this tell by reviewing games that are still ongoing.

This tell is not that important to learn, especially if you are just a beginner in Mafia, but it is a good tool to understand, as people occasionally use it and you may want to debate over that read given your opinion about it.

Meta is simply the process of reviewing previous games that a person played in, and comparing their behavior in the previous game with the current game you are playing. It is best if you compare a game where this person was town and a game where this person was scum, so that you can get the full spectrum of their habits as both alignments.

For example, if a person was scum in another game and tunneled (basically accused someone hard and stuck with their scumread for a long time) and they are tunneling in the ongoing game, you can use this meta analysis to support any scumreads you have on them.

However, your reason for comparison must be significant, not just one post or a single analysis where he/she made specific reads that also happen to be the same in the ongoing game.


Practically 90% of all reads form every read in Mafia, with some different combinations here and there. There are other "tells", however I disagree that these are actually true scumtells when used.

i. Gut reads
Literally making a opinion on someone based on nothing, or very little evidence. These types of reads are almost always just random thoughts based on nothing, so pay them little heed when they are presented.


ii. Tone reads
Practically the same as gut reads in every way, shape, and form, however they are based on "tone of voice." Tone usually isn't AI unless its AtE (I'll cover this in another hide tag), so these kinds of reads are usually worthless too.


iii. >randtown/>randscum
"randtown" and "randscum" represent the chance that someone has randed town or randed scum. >randtown/>randscum represent that someone thinks that a player is has a greater chance of being town or scum, but this chance is usually a small improvement, so reads that have >randtown/>randscum attached to them are not supposed to be taken very seriously.


iv. GtH
Gun-to-Head, or GtH, is making snap decisions on who is town and who is scum. Most of the time, the majority of these reads have very little meaning to them and GtH isn't very effective in regular games. A more meaningful tell is that scum usually are the ones who initiate a GtH, because people think they are a easy point of discussion to base off of.


v. Behavioral patterns that did not originate from a mafia game
This kind of read is rarer, since most Mafia players rarely play with anyone else in the forum outside of Mafia, but I noticed it in a couple of newbie games. If you aren't studying meta, then behavioral patterns are NAI when considering external sources. For example, if your friend plays games in Island of the Lost or some other AoPS forum, their tone there is not applicable as a read. This is because Mafia is a extremely unique game which requires people to change how they talk and speak to others in the game, which means that your friend will likely not use a tone that they would use in IOTL.


AtE

AtE, or Appeal to Emotion, is being extremely emotional about your position in the game and sometimes players self-vote and give up trying to save themselves from being hanged. Reading AtE is similar to NKA, it can be a massive WIFOM minefield, so tread carefully when analyzing AtE!

What to look for in AtE
There are significant differences between town AtE and scum AtE. It's hard to describe all of it in words, as the tone is usually the key to reading AtE, but I'll give a few pointers on what to look for:

- Meta does play a part in AtE, as people who AtE often have easy patterns you can see when comparing different games.

- Newbies who are town that AtE usually involve self-voting and then going non-existent. I personally think it is rare for newbies who are town to really go all out to defend themselves. These newbies who are extremely concerned about self-preservation and image are almost always scum.

- Town AtE in general varies from player to player. The most important actions to look for are people who getting genuinely frustrated and then start gloating over the fact that they got hanged. How do you determine when someone is genuinely frustrated? It's hard to describe, and it really depends on their tone and how they refute the reasons for why people are hanging them.

- Experienced players rarely AtE, so if one does, keep a wary eye on them. AtE is also the last resort for scum as well, and most experienced players who AtE are trying to barely squeeze their way out from the edge of the cliff.

- Scum who AtE get really emotional and sometimes go overboard with the amount of emotion they try to put into their posts. For example, if someone dramatically overreacts and continually self-votes, they are likely scum. Again, meta exceptions apply, but just by analyzing AtE, people who do these kinds of things are usually scum.


Commonly used Mafia Terms - Use this to pinpoint terms that you don't understand
Created by Mafia Universe
Edited by EulerNTM
Number terms
#-Shot - When a role can only be used a certain amount of times during a game, with # representing that number.

3P - Third Party - Players that are not aligned with the town or mafia.

A
AI - Alignment Indicative - an action or post by a player that IS indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia

Angleshooting - Angleshooting or to "angleshoot" is violating the spirit of the rules without actually breaking the rules. Angleshooting is not technically cheating, but is considered equally unacceptable. Explained in depth on the forum rules post.

Anti-spew - Usually used to refer to when a mafia-aligned player is very close to being killed and goes into “anti-spew”, where they are careful about their wording so that town are not able to determine who is/isn’t their partner(s) after they flip mafia.

AtE - Appeal to Emotion - When a player makes a post in order to evoke sympathy so that they can avoid being hanged.

B
Bandwagon - When player(s) hop onto a vote wagon.

BM - Short for "Bastard Mod", who can lie to players about game mechanics (including in their role PM), uses a relatively screwed setup where players might change roles, alliances, and sometimes with messed up, new roles. Generally not good for a newbie game.

BP - Bulletproof - When a player is immune to attack(s) during the night phase or during ITAs, either because they have a bulletproof modifier or a bulletproof vest.

BPV - Bulletproof Vest - A vest which is bulletproof, usually only takes one shot.

Breadcrumbing - When a player leaves clues as to what their role is/what their night actions are, in case they die without the information being revealed.

Bussing - when a mafia-aligned player votes or pushes a fellow mafia-aligned player in order to appear as if they are town-aligned whenever the mafia-aligned player they are voting has their role revealed.

C
CC - Counterclaim - when a player claims the same role as another player.

Cemetery - a place where dead players can talk about the game freely. Also known as the graveyard, DVC, or Dead Villager Chat.

CFD - Chinese Fire Drill - When a group of players suddenly switch their vote somewhere else at last minute, usually another player who wasn’t seen as an option for the hang at that point in the phase.

Claim Fishing - When a player tries to get another player to reveal their role, usually done in a subtle fashion.

Crossvoting - when two people are voting on each other. Usually in reference to MYLO or LYLO states where if Person A is voted by Person B, then either the game is over if they're both town (as mafia-aligned players can vote over Player B with majority) or there's confirmed a mafia-aligned player between the two of them.

Counterwagon - the person with the second highest amount of votes other than the person who was hanged, or in larger or more intricate games a person with enough votes on them to be considered in contention for the hang.

D
D1/D2… etc. - Day phase

DADV - Dead Air, Dead Villager - When communication in the thread has gone down, so players infer that it’s because the hang is a villager.

Derpclear - When someone seemingly shows a lack of knowledge about an open/semi-open setup, or any other information that a mafia-aligned player would be expected to know, and is then cleared for this reason by another player.

Distancing - When two mafia-aligned players try to make it seem like they are not aligned with each other.

DVC - Dead Villager Chat, otherwise known as the graveyard or cemetery. Despite the name, dead wolves are generally allowed to participate.

E
EOD - End of Day

F
F3/F5/F# - A point in the game at which # of players are alive. Generally, but not always, either used to describe a LYLO or MYLO situation or one where there is otherwise significantly less players alive than there were at game start.

Fakeclaiming - When a player claims a role that they do not have.

Flavor - The game’s theme, sometimes what roles are based off of, and creates a story usually based on what happens in the game.

Fluffposting/Fluffing - When a player posts nothing of content.

FPS - Fancy Play Syndrome - When a player uses an elaborate strategy over a more simple one.

Frozen - When a player has stopped posting, often after being called out as mafia in the thread.
Alternative: Thread is frozen and no one is posting.

G
Gambit - Any play which is made in order to evoke a certain reaction, which can be made from both alignments.

Gamethrowing - When a player actively makes an action to intentionally throw the game for their alignment; examples of this can include mafia hardclaiming mafia without a strategic reason and/or revealing who the mafia team is in the thread.

GOAT - Greatest of All Time

H
Hammer - A vote that’s placed on a player which causes the wagon to reach majority in order to hang the player that the wagon is on, ending the day phase after the vote is placed.

Hard Claim - to claim a role with there being no uncertain terms - see Soft Claim, is relevant.

Hedging - When a player does not commit to a stance and stays on the fence regarding a topic, usually the alignment of a player.

Hydra - An account shared by two or more players that share a role and have their own private chat.

I
ISO - Isolating a specific player’s posts in order to look at all of them in one place.

ITA - In Thread Attack - A mechanic used in most mashes where every player has a X% hit rate shot they may use in the thread during designated times to attempt to kill someone.

ITT - In The Thread

J
JOAT - Jack Of All Trades - A role where there are multiple (usually 1-use actions) that the player can use. Can be either mafia-aligned or town-aligned.

K
KP - Kill Power - A role of either town or mafia alignment that has the power to kill another player at night.

L
LHF - Low Hanging Fruit - A player relatively easy to get hang as either alignment.

Lolcatting - When a mafia-aligned player is confirmed mafia, often mechanically through night actions, and starts posting random stuff.

LYLO - Hang or Lose - A game state where the town must hang a non-town-aligned player or they lose. This is because the town has majority by only one player.

M
Meta - Meta overall refers to the shared history and knowledge of each other's playing styles that comes from having played with each other across several games. A meta read is when someone relies on their knowledge of another player's past behavior in a certain alignment in order to form an informed read.

Modkill - When a player is killed by the host or moderator for rule breaking during a game. This can include breaking the rules on OGI, angleshooting, or behavioral issues.

Multi - A secondary account of someone else. Any multi's found on AoPS are permabanned.

Multi-ISO - Isolating the posts of multiple people so that only the posts of the selected people are outputted.

MYLO - Mishang and Lose - A gamestate where the town must hang a mafia-aligned player or vote for no hang in order not to lose. Town has majority by two players in this gamestate.

N
N1/N2… etc. - Night phase

NAI - Not Alignment Indicative - an action or post by a player which is not indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia.

NK - Night Kill - The player(s) who were killed that night, either by mafia-aligned players or town-aligned players with killing power.

NL - No Hang - When no player is hang at the end of the day phase. A common strategy used during MYLO to force the mafia to kill another town member, narrowing down the pool of suspects.

O
Openwolving - When a player talks in a way that obviously indicates mafia behaviour.

OMGUS - Oh My Gosh You Suck - When a player votes someone because the other person voted them.

P
Peek - When a town investigative uses their night action on another player, and gets information on their alignment at the end of the night phase.

Pocketing - when a player tries to gain the townread of another player, usually made by mafia attempting to pocket town

POE - Process of Elimination - A strategy which attempts to identify players who aren’t cleared, and hang amongst those uncleared players in order to find mafia-aligned players. A list of who one wishes to hang are also often referred to as PoE.

PR - Power Role - A role that is not vanilla, and has special abilities in the setup. Can refer to both mafia-aligned and town-aligned players.

Q
N/A

R
Roleclaim - To claim your role in the thread, and is used as a tactic to avoid a hang or reveal that you are a PR.

RVS - Random Voting Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get the game started by randomly voting until solid evidence builds up. Often lasts until the middle of the first day phase, sometimes ends sooner.

RQS - Random Questioning Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get general information about players, like gender or past games. Often ends after all players have answered the questions, sometimes ends sooner.

RWSO - Rand Wolf, Sub Out - A belief that a player who rands mafia, or a specific player in general, is more likely to sub out after being randed in a mafia-aligned slot, when compared to being randed as a town aligned slot. Note that subbing out in a way that is impacted by alignment is against site rules, and so is speculation on why someone subbed out; people generally use this in a non-serious manner, and you shouldn’t be using it in a game.

S
Scum - Another word for mafia-aligned players

Scumbaiting - When a town-aligned player purposely acts like a PR in order to attract the night kill.

Scumhunting - When a player tries to deduce which players are mafia-aligned.

SOD - Start of Day

Soft Claim - to imply that you are a role without directly stating it in the thread. Also can be considered breadcrumbing - see Hard Claim, semi-relevant.

Spewing - Used to refer to when a flipped mafia-aligned player has given away information about who is or isn’t a member of the mafia (or when they're doing it while still alive).

T
Thunderdome - Two players attempting to get each other hanged with at least one attempting to force the game to hang between the two until one of them flips mafia, often a term used in LYLO where two players have already crossvoted; can also be shortened to "tdome".

Tinfoil - A theory or thought that relies on something highly unlikely being the case, often entertained or brought up out of paranoia.

TMI - Too Much Information - This is used as reasoning for someone being a wolf. It revolves around the idea that the post(s) the person is making are too well-informed, so they must be mafia since the mafia already know who the rest of the mafia are.

Tunnel - When a player is focusing on one other player, accusing only them of being scum and generally not trying to solve the rest of the game.

TWTBAW - Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf - This concept is where one person appears to be so suspicious that they seem too suspicious to be mafia-aligned, hence being Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf. This is often attributed to the fact that they would be more self-conscious about being so wolfy, and would be attempting to appear more like a town member if they were actually mafia-aligned.

U
N/A

V
VCA - Vote Count Analysis - Vote count analysis is a common strategy using previous votecounts to try to find town or scum, typically looking at player composition of wagons on certain players and what those players flipped, or if they have changed their vote to save certain players.

VT - Vanilla Townie - A town role with no special abilities.

Vanilla - A role with no special abilities (both Mafia Goon and Vanilla Townie fall under this category)

Villager - Another word for town-aligned player.

W
Wagon - Refers to the number of votes a player has that makes them a candidate for the hang.

WIFOM - Wine In Front Of Me - Term originating from this scene in The Princess Bride movie. Used to denote when someone is trying to guess or reason what choice a person made, when that person knew in advance that his/her choices would be subject to scrutiny.

WIL - Wants It Less - Refers to when players are showing a lack of desire to win. Often jokingly or mockingly used to denote players who played poorly.

WIM - Wants It More - Refers to the attribute of extreme passion/desire to win that some players possess.

WOAT - Worst of All Time

Wolf - Another word for mafia-aligned players.

X
N/A

Y
N/A

Z
N/A

Hello newbies! I'm here to help you guys out with this game.

[list]

And that's it! Let the games begin.

This tell is not that important to learn, especially if you are just a beginner in Mafia, but it is a good tool to understand, as people occasionally use it and you may want to debate over that read given your opinion about it. 

Meta is simply the process of reviewing previous games that a person played in, and comparing their behavior in the previous game with the current game you are playing. It is best if you compare a game where this person was town and a game where this person was scum, so that you can get the full spectrum of their habits as both alignments. 

For example, if a person was scum in another game and tunneled (basically accused someone hard and stuck with their scumread for a long time) and they are tunneling in the ongoing game, you can use this meta analysis to support any scumreads you have on them.

However, your reason for comparison must be significant, not just one post or a single analysis where he/she made specific reads that also happen to be the same in the ongoing game.
[/hide]

Practically 90% of all reads form every read in Mafia, with some different combinations here and there. There are other "tells", however I disagree that these are actually true scumtells when used.

i. [hide=Gut reads]Literally making a opinion on someone based on nothing, or very little evidence. These types of reads are almost always just random thoughts based on nothing, so pay them little heed when they are presented.[/hide]

ii. [hide=Tone reads]Practically the same as gut reads in every way, shape, and form, however they are based on "tone of voice." Tone usually isn't AI unless its AtE (I'll cover this in another hide tag), so these kinds of reads are usually worthless too.[/hide]

iii. [hide=>randtown/>randscum]"randtown" and "randscum" represent the chance that someone has randed town or randed scum. >randtown/>randscum represent that someone thinks that a player is has a greater chance of being town or scum, but this chance is usually a small improvement, so reads that have >randtown/>randscum attached to them are not supposed to be taken very seriously.[/hide]

iv. [hide=GtH]Gun-to-Head, or GtH, is making snap decisions on who is town and who is scum. Most of the time, the majority of these reads have very little meaning to them and GtH isn't very effective in regular games. A more meaningful tell is that scum usually are the ones who initiate a GtH, because people think they are a easy point of discussion to base off of.[/hide]

v. [hide=Behavioral patterns that did not originate from a mafia game]This kind of read is rarer, since most Mafia players rarely play with anyone else in the forum outside of Mafia, but I noticed it in a couple of newbie games. [b]If you aren't studying meta, then behavioral patterns are NAI when considering external sources.[/b] For example, if your friend plays games in Island of the Lost or some other AoPS forum, their tone there is not applicable as a read. This is because Mafia is a extremely unique game which requires people to change how they talk and speak to others in the game, which means that your friend will likely not use a tone that they would use in IOTL.[/hide]

[b]AtE[/b]

AtE, or Appeal to Emotion, is being extremely emotional about your position in the game and sometimes players self-vote and give up trying to save themselves from being hanged. Reading AtE is similar to NKA, it can be a massive WIFOM minefield, so tread carefully when analyzing AtE!

[hide=What to look for in AtE]
There are significant differences between town AtE and scum AtE. It's hard to describe all of it in words, as the tone is usually the key to reading AtE, but I'll give a few pointers on what to look for:

- Meta does play a part in AtE, as people who AtE often have easy patterns you can see when comparing different games.

- Newbies who are town that AtE usually involve self-voting and then going non-existent. I personally think it is rare for newbies who are town to really go all out to defend themselves. These newbies who are extremely concerned about self-preservation and image are almost always scum.

- Town AtE in general varies from player to player. The most important actions to look for are people who getting genuinely frustrated and then start gloating over the fact that they got hanged. How do you determine when someone is genuinely frustrated? It's hard to describe, and it really depends on their tone and how they refute the reasons for why people are hanging them. 

- Experienced players rarely AtE, so if one does, keep a wary eye on them. AtE is also the last resort for scum as well, and most experienced players who AtE are trying to barely squeeze their way out from the edge of the cliff.

- Scum who AtE get really emotional and sometimes go overboard with the amount of emotion they try to put into their posts. For example, if someone dramatically overreacts and continually self-votes, they are likely scum. Again, meta exceptions apply, but just by analyzing AtE, people who do these kinds of things are usually scum. 
[/hide]
[/hide]

[hide=Commonly used Mafia Terms - Use this to pinpoint terms that you don't understand]
Created by Mafia Universe
Edited by EulerNTM
[hide=Number terms]

[b]#-Shot[/b] - When a role can only be used a certain amount of times during a game, with # representing that number.

[b]3P[/b] - [b]Third Party[/b] - Players that are not aligned with the town or mafia.[/hide]
[hide=A]

[b]AI[/b] - [b]Alignment Indicative[/b] - an action or post by a player that IS indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia

[b]Angleshooting[/b] - Angleshooting or to "angleshoot" is violating the spirit of the rules without actually breaking the rules. Angleshooting is not technically cheating, but is considered equally unacceptable. Explained in depth on the forum rules post.

[b]Anti-spew[/b] - Usually used to refer to when a mafia-aligned player is very close to being killed and goes into “anti-spew”, where they are careful about their wording so that town are not able to determine who is/isn’t their partner(s) after they flip mafia.

[b]AtE[/b] - [b]Appeal to Emotion[/b] - When a player makes a post in order to evoke sympathy so that they can avoid being hanged.[/hide]
[hide=B]

[b]Bandwagon[/b] - When player(s) hop onto a vote wagon.

[b]BM[/b] - Short for "Bastard Mod", who can lie to players about game mechanics (including in their role PM), uses a relatively screwed setup where players might change roles, alliances, and sometimes with messed up, new roles. Generally not good for a newbie game.

[b]BP[/b] - [b]Bulletproof[/b] - When a player is immune to attack(s) during the night phase or during ITAs, either because they have a bulletproof modifier or a bulletproof vest.

[b]BPV[/b] - [b]Bulletproof Vest[/b] - A vest which is bulletproof, usually only takes one shot.

[b]Breadcrumbing[/b] - When a player leaves clues as to what their role is/what their night actions are, in case they die without the information being revealed.

[b]Bussing[/b] - when a mafia-aligned player votes or pushes a fellow mafia-aligned player in order to appear as if they are town-aligned whenever the mafia-aligned player they are voting has their role revealed.[/hide]
[hide=C]
[b]CC[/b] - [b]Counterclaim[/b] - when a player claims the same role as another player.

[b]Cemetery[/b] - a place where dead players can talk about the game freely. Also known as the graveyard, DVC, or Dead Villager Chat.

[b]CFD[/b] - [b]Chinese Fire Drill[/b] - When a group of players suddenly switch their vote somewhere else at last minute, usually another player who wasn’t seen as an option for the hang at that point in the phase.

[b]Claim Fishing[/b] - When a player tries to get another player to reveal their role, usually done in a subtle fashion.

[b]Crossvoting[/b] - when two people are voting on each other. Usually in reference to MYLO or LYLO states where if Person A is voted by Person B, then either the game is over if they're both town (as mafia-aligned players can vote over Player B with majority) or there's confirmed a mafia-aligned player between the two of them.

[b]Counterwagon[/b] - the person with the second highest amount of votes other than the person who was hanged, or in larger or more intricate games a person with enough votes on them to be considered in contention for the hang.
[/hide]
[hide=D]

[b]D1/D2… etc.[/b] - Day phase

[b]DADV[/b] - [b]Dead Air, Dead Villager[/b] - When communication in the thread has gone down, so players infer that it’s because the hang is a villager.

[b]Derpclear[/b] - When someone seemingly shows a lack of knowledge about an open/semi-open setup, or any other information that a mafia-aligned player would be expected to know, and is then cleared for this reason by another player.

[b]Distancing[/b] - When two mafia-aligned players try to make it seem like they are not aligned with each other.

[b]DVC[/b] - Dead Villager Chat, otherwise known as the graveyard or cemetery. Despite the name, dead wolves are generally allowed to participate.[/hide]
[hide=E]

[b]EOD[/b] - End of Day[/hide]
[hide=F]

[b]F3/F5/F#[/b] - A point in the game at which # of players are alive. Generally, but not always, either used to describe a LYLO or MYLO situation or one where there is otherwise significantly less players alive than there were at game start.

[b]Fakeclaiming[/b] - When a player claims a role that they do not have.

[b]Flavor[/b] - The game’s theme, sometimes what roles are based off of, and creates a story usually based on what happens in the game.

[b]Fluffposting/Fluffing[/b] - When a player posts nothing of content.

[b]FPS[/b] - [b]Fancy Play Syndrome[/b] - When a player uses an elaborate strategy over a more simple one.

[b]Frozen[/b] - When a player has stopped posting, often after being called out as mafia in the thread.
Alternative: Thread is frozen and no one is posting.
[/hide]
[hide=G]

[b]Gambit[/b] - Any play which is made in order to evoke a certain reaction, which can be made from both alignments.

[b]Gamethrowing[/b] - When a player actively makes an action to intentionally throw the game for their alignment; examples of this can include mafia hardclaiming mafia without a strategic reason and/or revealing who the mafia team is in the thread.

[b]GOAT[/b] - Greatest of All Time[/hide]
[hide=H]

[b]Hammer[/b] - A vote that’s placed on a player which causes the wagon to reach majority in order to hang the player that the wagon is on, ending the day phase after the vote is placed.

[b]Hard Claim[/b] - to claim a role with there being no uncertain terms - see Soft Claim, is relevant.

[b]Hedging[/b] - When a player does not commit to a stance and stays on the fence regarding a topic, usually the alignment of a player.

[b]Hydra[/b] - An account shared by two or more players that share a role and have their own private chat.[/hide]
[hide=I]

[b]ISO[/b] - Isolating a specific player’s posts in order to look at all of them in one place.

[b]ITA[/b] - [b]In Thread Attack[/b] - A mechanic used in most mashes where every player has a X% hit rate shot they may use in the thread during designated times to attempt to kill someone.

[b]ITT[/b] - In The Thread[/hide]
[hide=J]

[b]JOAT[/b] - [b]Jack Of All Trades[/b] - A role where there are multiple (usually 1-use actions) that the player can use. Can be either mafia-aligned or town-aligned.[/hide]
[hide=K]

[b]KP[/b] - [b]Kill Power[/b] - A role of either town or mafia alignment that has the power to kill another player at night.[/hide]
[hide=L]

[b]LHF[/b] - [b]Low Hanging Fruit[/b] - A player relatively easy to get hang as either alignment.

[b]Lolcatting[/b] - When a mafia-aligned player is confirmed mafia, often mechanically through night actions, and starts posting random stuff.

[b]LYLO[/b] - [b]Hang or Lose[/b] - A game state where the town must hang a non-town-aligned player or they lose. This is because the town has majority by only one player.[/hide]
[hide=M]

[b]Meta[/b] - Meta overall refers to the shared history and knowledge of each other's playing styles that comes from having played with each other across several games. A meta read is when someone relies on their knowledge of another player's past behavior in a certain alignment in order to form an informed read.

[b]Modkill[/b] - When a player is killed by the host or moderator for rule breaking during a game. This can include breaking the rules on OGI, angleshooting, or behavioral issues.

[b]Multi[/b] - A secondary account of someone else. Any multi's found on AoPS are permabanned.

[b]Multi-ISO[/b] - Isolating the posts of multiple people so that only the posts of the selected people are outputted.

[b]MYLO[/b] - Mishang and Lose - A gamestate where the town must hang a mafia-aligned player or vote for no hang in order not to lose. Town has majority by two players in this gamestate.[/hide]
[hide=N]

[b]N1/N2… etc.[/b] - Night phase

[b]NAI[/b] - [b]Not Alignment Indicative[/b] - an action or post by a player which is not indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia.

[b]NK[/b] - [b]Night Kill[/b] - The player(s) who were killed that night, either by mafia-aligned players or town-aligned players with killing power.

[b]NL[/b] - [b]No Hang[/b] - When no player is hang at the end of the day phase. A common strategy used during MYLO to force the mafia to kill another town member, narrowing down the pool of suspects.[/hide]
[hide=O]

[b]Openwolving[/b] - When a player talks in a way that obviously indicates mafia behaviour.

[b]OMGUS[/b] - [b]Oh My Gosh You Suck[/b] - When a player votes someone because the other person voted them.[/hide]
[hide=P]

[b]Peek[/b] - When a town investigative uses their night action on another player, and gets information on their alignment at the end of the night phase.

[b]Pocketing[/b] - when a player tries to gain the townread of another player, usually made by mafia attempting to pocket town

[b]POE[/b] - [b]Process of Elimination[/b] - A strategy which attempts to identify players who aren’t cleared, and hang amongst those uncleared players in order to find mafia-aligned players. A list of who one wishes to hang are also often referred to as PoE.

[b]PR[/b] - [b]Power Role[/b] - A role that is not vanilla, and has special abilities in the setup. Can refer to both mafia-aligned and town-aligned players.[/hide]
[hide=Q]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=R]

[b]Roleclaim[/b] - To claim your role in the thread, and is used as a tactic to avoid a hang or reveal that you are a PR.

[b]RVS[/b] - [b]Random Voting Stage[/b] - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get the game started by randomly voting until solid evidence builds up. Often lasts until the middle of the first day phase, sometimes ends sooner.

[b]RQS[/b] - [b]Random Questioning Stage[/b] - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get general information about players, like gender or past games. Often ends after all players have answered the questions, sometimes ends sooner.

[b]RWSO[/b] - [b]Rand Wolf, Sub Out[/b] - A belief that a player who rands mafia, or a specific player in general, is more likely to sub out after being randed in a mafia-aligned slot, when compared to being randed as a town aligned slot. Note that subbing out in a way that is impacted by alignment is against site rules, and so is speculation on why someone subbed out; people generally use this in a non-serious manner, and you shouldn’t be using it in a game.[/hide]
[hide=S]

[b]Scum[/b] - Another word for mafia-aligned players

[b]Scumbaiting[/b] - When a town-aligned player purposely acts like a PR in order to attract the night kill.

[b]Scumhunting[/b] - When a player tries to deduce which players are mafia-aligned.

[b]SOD[/b] - Start of Day

[b]Soft Claim[/b] - to imply that you are a role without directly stating it in the thread. Also can be considered breadcrumbing - see Hard Claim, semi-relevant.

[b]Spewing[/b] - Used to refer to when a flipped mafia-aligned player has given away information about who is or isn’t a member of the mafia (or when they're doing it while still alive).[/hide]
[hide=T]

[b]Thunderdome[/b] - Two players attempting to get each other hanged with at least one attempting to force the game to hang between the two until one of them flips mafia, often a term used in LYLO where two players have already crossvoted; can also be shortened to "tdome".

[b]Tinfoil[/b] - A theory or thought that relies on something highly unlikely being the case, often entertained or brought up out of paranoia.

[b]TMI[/b] - Too Much Information - This is used as reasoning for someone being a wolf. It revolves around the idea that the post(s) the person is making are too well-informed, so they must be mafia since the mafia already know who the rest of the mafia are.

[b]Tunnel[/b] - When a player is focusing on one other player, accusing only them of being scum and generally not trying to solve the rest of the game.

[b]TWTBAW[/b] - Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf - This concept is where one person appears to be so suspicious that they seem too suspicious to be mafia-aligned, hence being Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf. This is often attributed to the fact that they would be more self-conscious about being so wolfy, and would be attempting to appear more like a town member if they were actually mafia-aligned.[/hide]
[hide=U]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=V]

[b]VCA[/b] - [b]Vote Count Analysis[/b] - Vote count analysis is a common strategy using previous votecounts to try to find town or scum, typically looking at player composition of wagons on certain players and what those players flipped, or if they have changed their vote to save certain players.

[b]VT[/b] - [b]Vanilla Townie[/b] - A town role with no special abilities.

[b]Vanilla[/b] - A role with no special abilities (both Mafia Goon and Vanilla Townie fall under this category)

[b]Villager[/b] - Another word for town-aligned player.[/hide]
[hide=W]

[b]Wagon[/b] - Refers to the number of votes a player has that makes them a candidate for the hang.

[b]WIFOM[/b] - [b]Wine In Front Of Me[/b] - Term originating from [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U_eZmEiyTo0]this scene in The Princess Bride movie[/url]. Used to denote when someone is trying to guess or reason what choice a person made, when that person knew in advance that his/her choices would be subject to scrutiny.

[b]WIL[/b] - [b]Wants It Less[/b] - Refers to when players are showing a lack of desire to win. Often jokingly or mockingly used to denote players who played poorly.

[b]WIM[/b] - [b]Wants It More[/b] - Refers to the attribute of extreme passion/desire to win that some players possess.

[b]WOAT[/b] - Worst of All Time

[b]Wolf[/b] - Another word for mafia-aligned players.[/hide]
[hide=X]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=Y]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=Z]

N/A[/hide]
[/hide]

NDMath wrote:
To Power Role(s)
Cop - If you get a guilty you'll claim it tomorrow and likely get killed the next night regardless of who it is, so to determine your target you must look at who is the most beneficial to get an innocent result on. Checking the person the mafia killed is a waste of a check. Investigating the person we hang tomorrow also isn't very beneficial as you'll have to claim sooner than ideal to save them.

Tracker - If the hang flips town, there are 2 mafia alive making your only useful result is tracking the person who commits the kill. If the hang flips mafia, read the part about the cop.

Doctor - You either wanna protect the other power role as a guard against worst scenario, or the player most likely to be night killed to block the night kill.

Jailkeeper - You are the major power role in this setup if randed and that + the nature of the jk makes there not really a wrong way to use your action. Main goal is to block the kill in some way.

1*BP - Sit tight.

Tracker - If the hang flips town, there are 2 mafia alive making your only useful result is tracking the person who commits the kill. If the hang flips mafia, read the part about the cop.

Doctor - You either wanna protect the other power role as a guard against worst scenario, or the player most likely to be night killed to block the night kill.

NDMath wrote:
To Power Role(s)
Cop - If you get a guilty you'll claim it tomorrow and likely get killed the next night regardless of who it is, so to determine your target you must look at who is the most beneficial to get an innocent result on. Checking the person the mafia killed is a waste of a check. Investigating the person we hang tomorrow also isn't very beneficial as you'll have to claim sooner than ideal to save them.

Tracker - If the hang flips town, there are 2 mafia alive making your only useful result is tracking the person who commits the kill. If the hang flips mafia, read the part about the cop.

Doctor - You either wanna protect the other power role as a guard against worst scenario, or the player most likely to be night killed to block the night kill.

Jailkeeper - You are the major power role in this setup if randed and that + the nature of the jk makes there not really a wrong way to use your action. Main goal is to block the kill in some way.

1*BP - Sit tight.

[quote="NDMath"][hide=To Power Role(s)]
Cop - If you get a guilty you'll claim it tomorrow and likely get killed the next night regardless of who it is, so to determine your target you must look at who is the most beneficial to get an innocent result on. Checking the person the mafia killed is a waste of a check. Investigating the person we hang tomorrow also isn't very beneficial as you'll have to claim sooner than ideal to save them.

Tracker - If the hang flips town, there are 2 mafia alive making your only useful result is tracking the person who commits the kill. If the hang flips mafia, read the part about the cop.

Doctor - You either wanna protect the other power role as a guard against worst scenario, or the player most likely to be night killed to block the night kill.

Jailkeeper - You are the major power role in this setup if randed and that + the nature of the jk makes there not really a wrong way to use your action. Main goal is to block the kill in some way.

1*BP - Sit tight.
[/hide][/quote]
